title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: British Military Says Cargo Ship Was Hit While Crossing Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary:
British Military Says Cargo Ship Was Hit While Crossing Strait of Hormuz. A cargo ship traveling through the Strait of Hormuz on a United Nations-approved route was hit by a projectile Thursday, the British military said, as several tankers made their way out of the channel using the same path.

The post British Military Says Cargo Ship Was Hit While Crossing Strait of Hormuz appeared first on <a rel="nofollow" hre. Al Jazeera: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked.

Al Jazeera reports that iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.

stories.title: Leaders and celebrities react after powerful quakes hit Venezuela
stories.summary:
Leaders and celebrities react after powerful quakes hit Venezuela. World leaders and Venezuelan celebrities reacted Thursday with messages of solidarity and offers of assistance after two powerful earthquakes shook the South American nation, killing at least 164 people, injuring more than 1,000 and trapping many beneath collapsed buildings.

Al Jazeera: Hundreds trapped beneath buildings a day after Venezuela’s twin earthquakes. Al Jazeera reports that emergency crews are racing to rescue hundreds trapped under collapsed buildings after earthquakes struck Venezuela.

stories.summary:

MS NOW pundits melted down Thursday over two Supreme Court rulings that expanded President Trump’s immigration powers, calling the decision to end Temporary Protected Status for hundreds of thousands of migrants “catastrophic.” “I have not read the opinion.

I have made it through the holding. This is the catastrophic outcome that we feared,” former U.S. ...

stories.title: Top secrets come spilling out
stories.summary:
Top secrets come spilling out.

In 1917, British analysts deciphered a coded message the German foreign minister sent to one of his country's diplomats vowing to begin "unrestricted submarine warfare" and seeking to win over Mexico with a promise to "reconquer the lost territory in Texas, New Mexico and Arizona" if the US entered the world war.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

links.headline: Top secrets come spilling out

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

stories.title: Supreme Court Strikes Down ‘Vampire Rule’ In Major Win For Gun Rights
stories.summary:
Supreme Court Strikes Down ‘Vampire Rule’ In Major Win For Gun Rights. The Supreme Court handed gun-rights advocates a major victory Thursday, striking down Hawaii’s controversial “vampire rule” that effectively banned lawful concealed carry on most private property unless owners explicitly granted permission.

In a 6-3 decision split along ideological lines, the court ruled that Hawaii’s law violated the Second Amendment, with Justice Samuel Alito writing for ... Breitbart: Another One Bites the Dust: SCOTUS Strikes Down Hawaii's Concealed Carry Gun Control.

Breitbart reports that the Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) released its decision in Wolford v. Lopez Thursday and ruled that Hawaii's law regarding concealed carry on private property violates the 2nd and 14th Amendments.

The post Another One Bites the Dust: SCOTUS Strikes Down Hawaii’s Concealed Carry Gun Control appe

stories.title: How Trump’s Big Day At SCOTUS Opens Door For Border Crackdown, Deportations
stories.summary:
How Trump’s Big Day At SCOTUS Opens Door For Border Crackdown, Deportations. President Donald Trump scored major victories in two Supreme Court decisions issued Thursday that will allow him to ramp up his ongoing crackdown on the border and illegal immigration.

The high court ruled that the federal government could turn away migrants seeking asylum at ports of entry, rejecting arguments that federal law requires officials to ... Breitbart: DHS Celebrates Arrests of More than 10,000 Illegal Alien Gang Members Since Trump Took Office.

stories.title: Connor Metcalfe on the Socceroos’ win over Turkey: ‘You couldn’t ask for a better start’ – video
stories.summary:

‘I didn't want to get a yellow [card] so then I just ended up going for the the classic knee-slide,’ Metcalfe told reporters 22 hours after the historic scenes in Vancouver. Although he didn’t get the best sleep, he said the Socceroos couldn't have asked for a better start.

stories.title: Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders open, but don’t expect a physical copy
stories.summary:
Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders open, but don’t expect a physical copy.

The blockbuster launch is expected to dwarf the box office takings of the year’s biggest movies with one industry analyst predicting it could make $1bn within an hour It is, quite simply, the most anticipated piece of entertainment since the Star Wars prequels and now, at last, you can reserve a copy.

At midnight last night, Rockstar opened preorders on Grand Theft Auto VI, the latest title in the epic open-world gangster adventure series, five months before its 19 November release date.

stories.title: ‘More relevant than making fires’: Explorer Scouts launch badges for AI and digital age
stories.summary:
‘More relevant than making fires’: Explorer Scouts launch badges for AI and digital age.

Content creation and online safety among new topics for 14- to 18-year-olds – but tweaks may be needed when social media ban comes in Scouts are introducing badges in content creation, digital communication and online safety after consulting nearly 3,000 teenagers who said they wanted skills to help them navigate a world increasingly shaped by AI, social media and digital technology.

The new Explorer Scout badges, part of the Scout movement’s first major overhaul in almost 25 year.

stories.title: Weatherwatch: How UK firm’s low-cost tech can warn of volcanic eruptions
stories.summary:
Weatherwatch: How UK firm’s low-cost tech can warn of volcanic eruptions. VolcanoTech’s sulphur dioxide detecting sensors are in already in use in a number of countries Weather forecasts now include air quality warnings and cities have networks of air quality sensors driving real-time maps online.

Similar air quality sensors can warn of an imminent volcanic eruption. Just as a fizzy drink releases carbon dioxide when the pressure is released, rising magma emits dissolved sulphur dioxide as it rises.

stories.title: AI helps read papyrus scroll burnt to crisp during Vesuvius eruption
stories.summary:
AI helps read papyrus scroll burnt to crisp during Vesuvius eruption. Previously hidden text revealed without unrolling scroll discusses stoic philosophy on ethics, art and human behaviour The surviving part of an ancient scroll that was burnt to a crisp when Mount Vesuvius erupted nearly 2,000 years ago has been virtually unwrapped and read with help from artificial intelligence.

Researchers uncovered 20 columns of previously hidden text covering more than a metre of charred papyrus without physically unrolling the scroll. The work discusses stoic.

stories.title: Francisco exits PAR; Gardo intensifies into severe tropical storm
stories.summary:
Francisco exits PAR; Gardo intensifies into severe tropical storm. MANILA, Philippines — Severe Tropical Storm Francisco (international name: “Mekkhala”) has exited Philippine area of responsibility (PAR) on Thursday evening, said the state weather bureau, According to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) in its 11 p.m. typhoon bulletin, Francisco was last located 705 kilometers (km) northeast of Itbayat, Batanes outside PAR.

Rappler: Gardo enters PAR, intensifies into severe tropical storm; Francisco exits. Rappler reports that severe Tropical Storm Gardo (Higos), the Philippines' seventh tropical cyclone for 2026, will not affect the country.

stories.title: Biz groups back probe into shuttered Misamis steel plant
stories.summary:

MANILA, Philippines — Several business organizations have thrown their support behind the government’s investigation into Philippine Sanjia Steel Corp., the Chinese-run firm whose Misamis Oriental plant was shuttered after radioactive materials were found within the facility.

stories.title: Ateneo, Smart unaware of Aurora activity until after tragedy — Remulla
stories.summary:
Ateneo, Smart unaware of Aurora activity until after tragedy — Remulla. MANILA, Philippines — Ateneo de Manila University and telecommunications company Smart Communications were unaware of the training activity in Aurora where student-athletes Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili drowned, Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said.

“Based on all testimonies, the activity was carried out without the knowledge of Ateneo de Manila University,” Remulla said in Filipino in. Philippine Daily Inquirer: DILG to seek HDO vs ex-Ateneo coach Tab Baldwin.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines — The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) will recommend that a hold departure order (HDO) be issued against resigned Ateneo de Manila University head coach Tab Baldwin, Secretary Jonvic Remulla said Friday.

“We will recommend an HDO,” Remulla said during a press briefing.

Meta pauses employee tracker for AI training amid privacy concerns.

About 1,600 workers signed petition against tool that tracked staff keystrokes, mouse clicks and computer screen content Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta has paused a program that tracked employees’ computer activity amid data privacy concerns and a staff backlash.
